subject: Work Class Portfolio Planning — predictable blend coverage across work types for autonomous sessions
title: Work Class Portfolio Planning — Navigator-Driven Balanced Session Composition
what: Defines Work Class Portfolio Planning: the mechanism by which Autopilot (AP) and Ozi receive a portfolio slice from Navigator that guarantees predictable blend coverage across work types (feature | maintenance | security | docs | performance | debt). Rather than always executing the highest-ROI lug, AP plans each session as a portfolio of N items balanced across work classes. Navigator enforces blend constraints so no single work class dominates across consecutive sessions. The portfolio slice is the input to AP's session_plan in the goal chain.
why: Pure ROI-ordered execution produces lopsided sessions: high-ROI features crowd out maintenance and debt work until the codebase degrades and ROI on all future work drops. Portfolio planning prevents this: it distributes attention across work classes each session, mimicking how a healthy engineering team allocates time. Without this, autonomous sessions become feature-factories that accumulate invisible maintenance debt. Predictable blend coverage is the unique WAI constraint — it is not the same as traditional multi-objective optimization because the blend must be predictable (stakeholder-observable) rather than just Pareto-efficient.
audience: Navigator — computes and returns portfolio slices on request
AP — receives portfolio slice and builds session_plan from it
Ozi — uses portfolio slices to compose nightly session batches
Mario — reviews portfolio blend via Navigator reports to ensure the allocation matches his priorities
use_cases: Session startup: AP requests portfolio slice for a 100k-token session; Navigator returns 5 items: 2 feature, 1 maintenance, 1 debt, 1 security — AP plans all 5 in sequence
Blend correction: last 3 sessions were all feature work; Navigator adjusts next slice to 50% maintenance/debt to rebalance
Consecutive session Ozi: Ozi dispatches 5 nightly sessions; Navigator distributes portfolio slices so the 5 sessions collectively hit the target blend percentages
Override: Mario specifies 'this week focus on security' — Navigator boosts security weight temporarily; portfolio slices reflect the override
Blend audit: Mario asks 'what was our blend last 30 sessions?'; Navigator returns actuals vs target blend percentages
patterns: Portfolio slice = ordered list: Navigator returns N items in execution order; AP executes them in that order; no reordering by AP
Blend target: configurable per spoke in WAI-Spoke/advisors/navigator/portfolio-config.json; defaults: feature=35%, maintenance=25%, debt=20%, security=10%, docs=5%, performance=5%
Blend accounting: Navigator tracks actuals per session in portfolio-history.json; uses actuals to compute correction in next slice
Session budget fit: Navigator filters items by model_fit and estimated_token_cost before including in slice; items too large for the budget are held for a dedicated session
Focus-lock override: items in a focus-locked initiative always fill their slot first before blend correction is applied

target_blend.feature: float 0-1 — must sum to 1.0 across all classes

portfolio_config.blend_window_sessions: int default 10 — how many recent sessions to average when computing actuals
portfolio_config.focus_lock_override: bool default true — focus-lock items always fill first

portfolio_slice_request.model_id: string — items must be model_fit compatible
portfolio_slice_request.force_blend_reset: bool default false — ignore actuals, return pure target blend

portfolio_slice_response.correction_applied: object — adjustments made to hit target

slice.title: string
slice.work_class: feature | maintenance | debt | security | docs | performance

items_held: lug_id — items not included due to budget or blend constraints; candidates for next session

sessions.blend_actual: object — {work_class: fraction} for this session only

constraints: target_blend values must sum to 1.0; Navigator rejects or normalizes configs that don't
AP must not reorder the portfolio slice — the order encodes blend and dependency decisions made by Navigator
Navigator must not include items from a focus-locked initiative that the AP session cannot complete within its token budget
blend_window_sessions must be at least 3 to produce meaningful actuals; single-session actuals are not corrective
work_class is a required field on all lug types; lugs without work_class are treated as debt for blend accounting purposes
Portfolio history is a spoke-side file; it is never sent to the hub (contains session-level operational data, not fleet intelligence)
tests: Target blend enforcement: 10 ready items all of class 'feature'; slice of 5 returns at most ceil(0.35 * 5)=2 feature items; remainder pulled from lower-ROI other-class items
Blend correction: last 5 sessions were 100% feature; next slice has 0 feature items until actuals recover toward target
Focus-lock priority: 1 focus-lock item + 4 feature items; focus-lock item is position=1 regardless of ROI
Budget fit: 1 item estimated at 150k tokens, session budget 100k; item appears in items_held, not in slice
blend sum validation: config with blend not summing to 1.0 — Navigator rejects with clear error
Portfolio history append: after session completes, blend_actual recorded matches completed lug work_classes

open_questions: work_class field: add to all existing lug types retroactively, or only require for new lugs going forward?
estimated_tokens: who computes this? Navigator estimating from lug size is inaccurate; should this come from a Haiku pre-scan of each lug's execute section?
Blend override: should Mario's 'focus on X this week' override be a temporary portfolio-config mutation or a separate priority boost mechanism?

perceive: Read this spec. Read WAI-Spoke/advisors/navigator/recommendations-current.json for current Navigator behavior. Check if WAI-Spoke/advisors/navigator/portfolio-config.json exists. Scan 10 recent lugs in bytype/ and note their work_class field (or absence).
execute: 1. Create WAI-Spoke/advisors/navigator/portfolio-config.json with default target blend. 2. Audit existing lugs for work_class field; add where missing. 3. Implement portfolio_slice() function in Navigator advisor: blend accounting from history + correction + focus-lock priority + budget fit. 4. Create WAI-Spoke/advisors/navigator/portfolio-history.json scaffold. 5. Wire portfolio_slice() into AP session startup after goal chain claim.
verify: 1. portfolio-config.json parses and target_blend sums to 1.0. 2. Slice of all-feature items returns at most target feature fraction. 3. Blend correction adjusts slice after lopsided history. 4. Focus-lock item appears first. 5. Over-budget item appears in items_held.
